groups.desc:
Questions 21-25
Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

questions.title: What skills does Peter want to learn?

options.title: C
options.content: how to write a business proposal

options.title: C
options.content: he can communicate well with many people

questions.title: What will the careers officer help Peter with?
Why did Peter choose the IT business?

options.title: A
options.content: to update his knowledge in the area

questions.title: What will Peter do for the next term?

groups.question_title: What job opportunities are the following companies offering?
groups.desc:
Questions 26-30
What job opportunities are the following companies offering?
Choose FIVE answers from the box and write the correct letter, A-G, next to Questions 26-30.

transcript.speaker: Peter
transcript.en: Hi, Shona.

transcript.speaker: Shona
transcript.en: Hi, Peter.

transcript.speaker: Shona
transcript.en: Have you made up your mind about which internship programme to apply for?

transcript.speaker: Peter
transcript.en: Yes, pretty much.

transcript.speaker: Shona
transcript.en: I'm entirely at a loss.

transcript.speaker: Shona
transcript.en: I mean, there are so many good ones.

transcript.speaker: Shona
transcript.en: Can you give me some tips?

transcript.speaker: Peter
transcript.en: Sure.

transcript.speaker: Shona
transcript.en: What is your top priority when weighing up these programmes?

transcript.speaker: Peter
transcript.en: Well, I guess I'd go for ones that can help me with the skill set I lack for my future career.

transcript.speaker: Peter
transcript.en: I still need a lot of practical training in dealing with statistics.

transcript.speaker: Peter
transcript.en: But keep in mind that you don't want to set your goal too grand, like drawing up a business plan.

transcript.speaker: Peter
transcript.en: You might end up nowhere.

transcript.speaker: Shona
transcript.en: I see.

transcript.speaker: Shona
transcript.en: So, I want to learn how to organise ideas and present them.

transcript.speaker: Peter
transcript.en: Good.

transcript.speaker: Peter
transcript.en: Now you've nailed step one.

transcript.speaker: Shona
transcript.en: Now what?

transcript.speaker: Peter
transcript.en: You must also consider your strengths for a suitable internship programme.

transcript.speaker: Peter
transcript.en: Otherwise, you might get rejected or end up with one that you are incapable of doing.

transcript.speaker: Shona
transcript.en: But I have no idea what I'm good at.

transcript.speaker: Peter
transcript.en: Communicate with people around you or whom you've worked with.

transcript.speaker: Peter
transcript.en: That's what I did.

transcript.speaker: Peter
transcript.en: I thought I was a good team leader, but many people I've worked with for various projects mentioned something else.

transcript.speaker: Peter
transcript.en: They admire that I could work around the clock to finish projects on time.

transcript.speaker: Peter
transcript.en: And indeed, I'm more focused and perform better when I'm pushed for time.

transcript.speaker: Shona
transcript.en: Good point.

transcript.speaker: Shona
transcript.en: So what do you plan to do next?

transcript.speaker: Peter
transcript.en: I'll visit the careers officer to help me with the school application form.

transcript.speaker: Peter
transcript.en: You know, the one we have to submit before applying to companies.

transcript.speaker: Shona
transcript.en: But it is unnecessary to fill it in.

transcript.speaker: Shona
transcript.en: It's only optional.

transcript.speaker: Peter
transcript.en: Really?

transcript.speaker: Peter
transcript.en: I'm glad I heard from you.

transcript.speaker: Peter
transcript.en: Then I'll ask the careers officer to look at my personal statement and give me some tips on polishing it up.

transcript.speaker: Shona
transcript.en: Don't you need help with your CV?

transcript.speaker: Peter
transcript.en: No, I'm good.

transcript.speaker: Peter
transcript.en: I have already learned to write it in class.

transcript.speaker: Shona
transcript.en: That's good.

transcript.speaker: Shona
transcript.en: So what kind of company did you choose?

transcript.speaker: Peter
transcript.en: I've decided to apply for an IT company.

transcript.speaker: Shona
transcript.en: Why is that?

transcript.speaker: Peter
transcript.en: Well, even though I've learned how the IT industry developed in the past from different lectures, I know nothing about its latest trends.

transcript.speaker: Peter
transcript.en: I'm prepared to work in an IT company and fill in the information gap.

transcript.speaker: Shona
transcript.en: I see.

transcript.speaker: Shona
transcript.en: What do you plan to do next term?

transcript.speaker: Peter
transcript.en: I think I'll stick to most of the optional modules I've chosen.

transcript.speaker: Peter
transcript.en: But regarding the language sessions, I'm doing poorly in Japanese.

transcript.speaker: Peter
transcript.en: I'd better drop it before wasting more time.

transcript.speaker: Peter
transcript.en: I think I'll learn Spanish instead.

transcript.speaker: Shona
transcript.en: Oh, I learned Spanish for a year.

transcript.speaker: Shona
transcript.en: I think you'll find it interesting.

transcript.speaker: Shona
transcript.en: Do you know what job opportunities these companies offer interns?

transcript.speaker: Peter
transcript.en: I've searched online and browsed some of the schemes.

transcript.speaker: Peter
transcript.en: Do you know SAIC?

transcript.speaker: Shona
transcript.en: Yes.

transcript.speaker: Shona
transcript.en: I heard they offer internship positions dealing with customer interaction management.

transcript.speaker: Shona
transcript.en: But I'm not quite sure what that means.

transcript.speaker: Peter
transcript.en: Well, the position offers the chance to become fully aware of customer value and keep in touch with customers to keep them highly engaged and satisfied.

transcript.speaker: Peter
transcript.en: These include activities such as PR, communications, marketing and customer service.

transcript.speaker: Shona
transcript.en: Sounds like loads of work to do.

transcript.speaker: Shona
transcript.en: What about Gemini Technologies?

transcript.speaker: Peter
transcript.en: It's an IT consulting company.

transcript.speaker: Peter
transcript.en: Although many employees say it's stressful working there, I want to apply for this one.

transcript.speaker: Shona
transcript.en: Why is that?

transcript.speaker: Peter
transcript.en: Actually, the company provides service to improve software architecture.

transcript.speaker: Peter
transcript.en: But that's pretty demanding as a team of software engineers has to provide detailed analysis and suggestions for required changes before the deadline.

transcript.speaker: Peter
transcript.en: As an observer and participant of a particular project, an intern can learn how to work to a strict deadline.

transcript.speaker: Shona
transcript.en: That's not my area of expertise.

transcript.speaker: Shona
transcript.en: But I'm sure you'll excel at this.

transcript.speaker: Peter
transcript.en: I certainly do hope so.

transcript.speaker: Peter
transcript.en: Have you checked out Deloitte?

transcript.speaker: Shona
transcript.en: Of course.

transcript.speaker: Shona
transcript.en: That's a renowned accounting firm, and thus its internships offer excellent learning experiences in this regard.

transcript.speaker: Shona
transcript.en: You know, things like examining a company's financial statements - the income statement, balance sheet and cash flow statement.

transcript.speaker: Shona
transcript.en: I want to give it a try.

transcript.speaker: Shona
transcript.en: But I might get rejected, so I need a backup plan.

transcript.speaker: Shona
transcript.en: Have you read anything about Kearney?

transcript.speaker: Peter
transcript.en: Yes.

transcript.speaker: Peter
transcript.en: It is another consulting firm.

transcript.speaker: Peter
transcript.en: It provides opportunities to reach out to other business partners and pay attention to networking.

transcript.speaker: Shona
transcript.en: Well, that sounds interesting.

transcript.speaker: Peter
transcript.en: I'm thinking about Vortex.

transcript.speaker: Peter
transcript.en: But I haven't got anything useful from its website.

transcript.speaker: Shona
transcript.en: I worked there last summer.

transcript.speaker: Shona
transcript.en: It's an international company with headquarters in Spain, so most of the managers here speak Spanish.

transcript.speaker: Shona
transcript.en: Thanks to the internship experience, I became fluent in it.

transcript.speaker: Peter
transcript.en: Right.

transcript.speaker: Peter
transcript.en: That's not for me.

21.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 skills；条件为 Peter。
题目解析：先听到定位词被替换为 skill set，随后听到“需要处理数据方面的实践训练”，为 B 选项的同义替换，可判断 B 选项为正确答案。之后虽听到 C 选项的“撰写商业计划书”，但男生说的是“不要将目标设得太高”，可排除该选项。最后女生补充说到 A 选项，但其并非题干要求的男生想要学习的技能，可排除该选项。

22.analysis:
听前预测：条件为 Peter's main strength。
题目解析：男生提及要女生先了解自身优势，女生表示不清楚。随后男生提到 C 选项的 communicate with people，但他只是建议女生“与身边的人沟通”，并不是说这是他的优势，可判断 C 选项偷换概念，可以排除。之后他又提及 A 选项，不过他又立刻否定，说合作过的人说他有其他长处，可排除该选项。最后男生提到别人夸他“能夜以继日地工作，按时完成项目”，对应 B 选项，可判断 B 选项为正确答案。

23.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 careers officer。
题目解析：定位词原词重现，随后听到 C 选项，但女生否定了该信息，说不必填写，可排除该选项。继续往下听，听到 B 选项搭配 careers officer 出现，可判断 B 选项为正确答案。最后女生提及 A 选项，但男生否定了该信息，可排除该选项。

24.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 IT business，条件为 Peter。
题目解析：先听到定位词被替换为 IT company，随后听到了“了解行业的最新趋势”，对应 A 选项，可判断 A 选项为正确答案。原文未提及 B 选项；原文虽提及 C 选项的 lectures，但表达的意思与该选项无关。

25.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 next term，条件为 Peter。
题目解析：定位词原词重现，随后听到三个选项的关键词 language，男生说他日语学得差，想放弃日语转学西班牙语，对应 B 选项，而非 C 选项。

26.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 SAIC。
题目解析：通过定位词的拼写预判其大致发音，便于定位。先听到定位词原词重现，随后听到女生说该职位“处理客户关系”，但她不确定具体是什么意思。男生补充道“该职位可以充分了解客户价值，与客户保持联系”，均为 B 选项的同义替换，可判断 B 选项为正确答案。注意，A 选项与 B 选项较为相似，具有一定的干扰性。二者的区别在于，A 选项强调 business contacts（业务联系），B 选项强调 customer，只有找出区分选项的核心信息，才能合理地判断所听信息。

27.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 Gemini Technologies。
题目解析：定位词原词重现，随后听到男生对该公司的看法，但和选项都对应不上。女生进一步提问后，听到男生提及“赶在截止日期前”“实习生可以学习如何在严格的期限内工作”，均为 C 选项的同义替换，可判断 C 选项为正确答案。

28.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 Deloitte。
题目解析：定位词原词重现，随后听到女生提及“那是一家著名的会计事务所，因此它的实习在这方面提供了极好的学习经历”，为 F 选项的具象化替换，后续的财务报表、损益表、资产负债表等也均为其的替换。

29.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 Kearney。
题目解析：定位词原词重现，随后听到男生提及“它提供接触其他业务伙伴的机会”，为 A 选项的同义替换。注意，该选项中的 business 是个多义词，此处意为“业务”。

30.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 Vortex。
题目解析：定位词原词重现，随后听到女生提及“它的总部在西班牙”，并非 E 选项“到另一个国家”。继续往下听，听到女生提到“大多数管理者都说西班牙语，因为这段实习经历，我的西班牙语变得很流利”，为 D 选项的同义替换，可判断 D 选项为正确答案。
